user_prompt: look at `lib\AudioFileRecorder`, implement what is written in the readme there
agent_reply: - Added `AudioFifo::dequeueMonoImmediate(...)` so recorder stop can drain FIFO contents even when normal watermark gating would hold data
Verification: `pio run` compiled `AudioFileRecorder.cpp` and the updated demo. The full build still fails at the existing unrelated `EspFileManager` dependency issue: missing `AsyncTCP.h`.

user_prompt: how easy is it to change `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp` to use SdFat instead of SD? What are the penalties if we made this change?
agent_reply: Pretty easy if you only mean `AudioFileRecorder.cpp`: Iâ€™d call it a small-to-medium change, not a rewrite.
The recorder uses `SD` in just a few places:

The main work is replacing Arduino `SD`/`File` with SdFatâ€™s `SdFs` or `SdFat` plus `FsFile`, then changing `grow_file()` and truncation to use SdFat APIs. The write path itself is simple: `g_file.write(...)`, `flush()`, `close()`, `seek()` all have close equivalents.

- **Compatibility penalty:** `File` becomes `FsFile`, so the public `grow_file(File&)` signature in `AudioFileRecorder.h` either changes or needs a wrapper. The demo code also calls `grow_file(File&)`, so that would need updating too.

- **VFS path penalty:** today `currentVfsPath()` returns `/sd/...` and `truncate()` works because Arduino `SD` is mounted into ESP-IDF VFS. Pure SdFat wonâ€™t automatically give you `/sd/...`, so anything expecting POSIX paths may break unless we preserve/mount a VFS layer or stop exposing that path.

- **GUI/shared SD penalty:** `FlyGui.cpp` also falls back to `SD.open()` for image files. If the card is owned by SdFat only, that fallback may no longer work unless we update it too or keep Arduino `SD` mounted separately.
- **Dependency/config penalty:** youâ€™ll add the SdFat library and choose config details. On ESP32/Core2, SPI settings and card type need a little care.
- **Upside:** better low-level file control, likely better preallocation/truncate options, and potentially more predictable high-throughput writes. That matters for audio recording.

My take: easy if the goal is â€œrecorder writes via SdFat.â€ Less easy if the app still wants `/sd/...` VFS paths and existing `SD` access elsewhere to keep working unchanged. Thatâ€™s the real catch.

The recorder uses `SD` in just a few places:

The main work is replacing Arduino `SD`/`File` with SdFatâ€™s `SdFs` or `SdFat` plus `FsFile`, then changing `grow_file()` and truncation to use SdFat APIs. The write path itself is simple: `g_file.write(...)`, `flush()`, `close()`, `seek()` all have close equivalents.

- **Compatibility penalty:** `File` becomes `FsFile`, so the public `grow_file(File&)` signature in `AudioFileRecorder.h` either changes or needs a wrapper. The demo code also calls `grow_file(File&)`, so that would need updating too.

- **VFS path penalty:** today `currentVfsPath()` returns `/sd/...` and `truncate()` works because Arduino `SD` is mounted into ESP-IDF VFS. Pure SdFat wonâ€™t automatically give you `/sd/...`, so anything expecting POSIX paths may break unless we preserve/mount a VFS layer or stop exposing that path.

- **GUI/shared SD penalty:** `FlyGui.cpp` also falls back to `SD.open()` for image files. If the card is owned by SdFat only, that fallback may no longer work unless we update it too or keep Arduino `SD` mounted separately.
- **Dependency/config penalty:** youâ€™ll add the SdFat library and choose config details. On ESP32/Core2, SPI settings and card type need a little care.
- **Upside:** better low-level file control, likely better preallocation/truncate options, and potentially more predictable high-throughput writes. That matters for audio recording.

My take: easy if the goal is â€œrecorder writes via SdFat.â€ Less easy if the app still wants `/sd/...` VFS paths and existing `SD` access elsewhere to keep working unchanged. Thatâ€™s the real catch.

user_prompt: I want a project wide removal of SD and we are changing to using SdFat instead. VFS paths are not important. What is important is that the files being created have actual creation time stamp file attributes so we can sort them later.
agent_reply: Implemented the project-wide SD to SdFat move.
- Updated GUI image SD fallback to use SdFat instead of Arduino `SD`: [FlyGui.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp)

Timestamp handling is now centralized through `FsDateTime::setCallback(...)`, using `ClockAgent` to populate FAT creation/sync timestamps via `FS_DATE` / `FS_TIME`. I also removed the VFS path API and POSIX `truncate()` usage; files are now truncated through `FsFile::truncate()` while still open.
I verified there are no remaining `#include <SD.h>` or `SD.` references. I couldnâ€™t run a PlatformIO build because `pio` is not installed/on PATH here, and `python -m platformio` is unavailable.

References used for SdFat timestamp/preallocation behavior: [SdFat GitHub](https://github.com/greiman/SdFat), [SdFat timestamp callback notes](https://github-wiki-see.page/m/renesas/Arduino/wiki/How-to-set-timestamps-on-files-handled-by-SdFat).

user_prompt: why does `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp` have a namespace nested in another namespace?
agent_reply: It has two namespaces for two different visibility levels:
The outer `namespace AudioFileRecorder` is the public module API declared in `AudioFileRecorder.h`, so callers use:

The inner unnamed namespace is a C++ trick for â€œprivate to this `.cpp` file.â€ Everything inside it has internal linkage, so symbols like `g_file`, `init_sd()`, `write_packet()`, `TAG`, etc. cannot collide with similarly named symbols in other files and are not part of the public API.
So itâ€™s roughly equivalent to saying:
- unnamed nested namespace: private state/helpers used only by this implementation file.
It is a common pattern for C++ modules.

user_prompt: edit `AudioFileRecorder::stopRecording()`, give it a boolean parameter called `estop`, if `etop` is true, flush and close the file immediately without pumping any more packets.
also add a getter and setter for `g_pure_pcm_mode`
agent_reply: - When `estop` is `true`, it disables queues, skips the FIFO drain/pump loop, then flushes, truncates to `g_bytes_written`, and closes the file.
Verified with `pio run`: build succeeded. There are unrelated existing warnings from dependencies and one existing packed-member warning in `AudioFileRecorder.cpp`.

user_prompt: // TODO: calculate the duration, add a mechanism to track average time (low pass filtered, publicly reports in floating point millisecond units), and maximum time (can be read and also reset from public calls)
agent_reply: Added timing stats around successful packet writes in [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:154):
- Low-pass filtered average write duration, alpha `0.05`
- Duration measured with `micros()` using wrapping-safe unsigned subtraction
- Public reporting in floating point milliseconds
Verified with `pio run`: build succeeded. Existing packed-member warning remains in `AudioFileRecorder.cpp`.

commit_message: file recorder, statistics, pure pcm mode, no debugging

user_prompt: modify `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp`, using `update_write_duration_stats`, have it remember the last time that the time exceeded 10ms.

Have two flags, one for whether or not the last call to write_packet exceeded that time, and one that latches true if it has exceeded, and something else has to reset the flag to false. Have public getters and resetters for these. Have a public getter for that timestamp too
At the start of `pump()`, if the last time the flush has happened was more than 2 seconds ago, call a `flush()` if the file is open
agent_reply: - Added last-call exceeded flag, latched exceeded flag, resetters, and timestamp getter.
- `pump()` now calls a timed flush helper at the start when the open recording file has not been flushed for over `2s`.
- Flush timestamp is updated after `flush()` returns.

Renamed the long-write API and internals in [AudioFileRecorder.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.h:40) and [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:98).
Internal state now uses `longwrite` naming too, like `g_longwrite`, `g_longwrite_latched`, and `g_last_longwrite_ms`.

commit_message: file recorder track when cache was flushed, and autoflushes

user_prompt: when called, it starts a new FreeRTOS task, the task then does everything
there needs to be a getter for status, a registerable callback for completion
the task will use the microSD card

rename the file "cloud_history.txt" to "cloud_history.old.txt" and then create a new "cloud_history.txt". It needs to simultaneously have both file handles open for "cloud_history.old.txt" and "cloud_history.txt"
then it walks through "cloud_history.old.txt" line by line
the lines could be a line representing a file, or a error log line, see `lib\WifiManager\CloudUpload.cpp`

if it is a file line, then take the file name, parse out the date from it using the established format used by `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp`. If the file mentioned is more than 3 days old, then we take further action, if not, write it back into the new "cloud_history.txt". So if the file mentioned is too old, delete it if it exists.
if it is a message line, it also has a date and time, so parse it out, if the time is less than 3 days old (use the same constant as before), then write it into the new "cloud_history.txt"
when parsing dates, use `bool parse_datetime(const char* text, m5::rtc_datetime_t& out)` from `utilfuncs.cpp`, this way you don't have to handle two different formats.
agent_reply: Implemented `AudioFileRecorder::prune_history()` and the surrounding status/callback API in [AudioFileRecorder.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.h:31) and [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:201).
- Starts a FreeRTOS task on the current core.
- Opens old/new history files at the same time.
- Keeps recent history lines, prunes old message lines, and deletes old uploaded recording files when possible.
- Uses `parse_datetime(...)` for both filename dates and history message timestamps.
- `pio run -e m5stack-core2` still fails at link with existing project-level issues: IRAM overflow and unresolved `__wrap_log_printf`, not from this change.

user_prompt: can you please move the pruning function in `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp` and the private related helpers to `lib\AudioFileRecorder\FileHistory.cpp` and `lib\AudioFileRecorder\FileHistory.h`?
agent_reply: Done. I moved the prune history API/types into [FileHistory.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/FileHistory.h) and the pruning task plus private helpers into [FileHistory.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/FileHistory.cpp).

[AudioFileRecorder.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.h) now includes `FileHistory.h`, so existing callers can still use `AudioFileRecorder::prune_history()` without changing call sites. [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp) no longer owns that pruning state machine.
Validation: `git diff --check` clean, and `pio run -e theflybringuptest` passed.

user_prompt: I've added a special `AUDSRC_META_TEXT` type of audio source, what it represents is not audio at all. Instead, when the decoder script encounters this, `file_packet_t`'s `payload` is a string of text (`payload_length` will be the length of the string).
This string of text is to be embedded in the output `.wav` file as a `INFO` `ICMT` chunk.

`AudioFileRecorder` needs a public call that can queue up a string to be embedded into the stream. It doesn't have to be inserted immediately, it's perfectly fine to insert it during `stopRecording`. Make sure it's thread safe.
Only one string can be queued, calls overwrite previously queued. The queue is cleared when the action is performed (saved to file).
So modify `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp`, `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.h`, and `scripts\thefly_audio_decoder.py` to do this
agent_reply: Implemented the metadata text path end to end.
- Stores one queued string, thread-safe behind `g_recorder_mutex`.
- Calls overwrite the previous queued string.
- Max stored payload is 512 bytes; longer strings are truncated and the call returns `false`.
- During `stopRecording()`, after optional FIFO drain and before flush/truncate/close, it writes an `AUDSRC_META_TEXT` packet and clears the queue once saved.
- Metadata packets are skipped for audio timing/PCM decoding.
- The payload is decoded as UTF-8 text.
- The output WAV gets a `LIST/INFO/ICMT` comment chunk appended.
- Synthetic `.rec` decode confirmed the WAV contains `LIST`, `INFO`, `ICMT`, and the comment text.

- Synthetic `.rec` decode confirmed the WAV contains `LIST`, `INFO`, `ICMT`, and the comment text.

commit_message: audio stream now embeds meta text

user_prompt: When a recording is finished inside AudioFileRecorder::stopRecording , use CallManager to generate a chunk of text to write into the file with AudioFileRecorder::queueMetaText if the recording was a call. If it was a memo, figure out what RecordingView says is the memo-type, use AudioFileRecorder::queueMetaText to make it say â€œmemo-type:<x>â€. If possible, rename the file so that the first letter captures what the memo type was.
agent_reply: The recorder now queues final metadata from inside `AudioFileRecorder::stopRecording`: calls get a `CallManager`-formatted text block, and memos get `memo-type:<x>`. The recording view now reports memo-type changes to the recorder, and stopped memo files are renamed after close so the first filename letter matches the final memo type when possible. Promoted memo-to-Bluetooth recordings are marked as call recordings.

Note: `pio run -e thefly` still fails because that env does not define `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL`; that is unrelated to this change. `src/version.c` was already dirty before I started, and the build script regenerated its hash.

commit_message: wired up the meta text stuff for recorded files

agent_reply: That crash is not the serial port. Itâ€™s an interrupt watchdog in the Bluedroid HFP client on core 0 during service-level connection. Core 1 was in `AudioFileRecorder::pump()` trying to take the recorder mutex, even though there may be no active recording.
I made the recorder pump path much less intrusive:
- Switched the recorder active flag to an atomic fast check in [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:66).
- `pump()` now immediately returns when no recording is active.
- [main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:345) now skips calling `pump()` entirely unless `needsPump()` is true.
This should stop the idle main loop from hammering FreeRTOS mutex/queue internals during Bluetooth pairing/HFP setup.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`: build succeeded. If the watchdog still fires on pairing, the next suspect is the temporary HFP debug build flags in `platformio.ini`.

user_prompt: ### Task: major change to recorded encrypted file format
The current implementation uses nearly standard `*.wav` files when unencrypted (lowest security level builds), this is easy to stream and play back.

The current implementation uses a custom format that uses fixed sized packets but each packet is variable length internally, and itâ€™s all encrypted. While this packet can also carry metadata and can be extended to carry event data, it is highly unlikely that the advantages of the flexibility is worth it over the disadvantage of it being difficult to play back in the same way, with easily calculatable audio durations and the ability to seek within a file. The file size is smaller but microSD card performance benchmarks shows that we do not need the smaller file size.
So we are going to throw out the whole custom format idea. We are just going to make encrypted `*.wav` files but call them `*.rec` files.
The document `developer-docs\recording-file-format.md` has been updated and is considered current.
A lot of stuff needs to be removed from `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp` , but the new `*.wav` writing functions only need very small changes for this to work.
There needs to be two static buffers, one for the size of a chunk of plaintext audio, and the second buffer for the encrypted audio, before it gets written to the file.

Remove `AudioFileDecoder.cpp` and the associated header file completely. Donâ€™t bother editing them to make them work. Benchmarks have shown that they are incredibly slow and the user will never use them. But we will offer live playback, which is fast because the bottleneck is actually just microSD card writing.
The sizes of the plaintext chunk and the result encrypted chunks shall be defined in `inc\defs.h` so the code can use them later, include also the encrypted RIFF header size.
Do not yet edit the playback related code, do not edit the python scripts. Weâ€™ll do that later
agent_reply: Implemented the encrypted recording format change.
- Added shared encrypted WAV chunk sizing in [inc/defs.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:12):
`WAV_ENCRYPTED_AUDIO_PLAINTEXT_LENGTH = 1024`, encrypted audio chunk length, and encrypted RIFF header length.

- Reworked [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:90) so security level 0 still writes normal `.wav`, while security level 1+ writes `.rec` as encrypted WAV-shaped chunks.
- Encrypted `.rec` files now write the RIFF header as the first encrypted chunk, then fixed-size encrypted PCM chunks.
- Final partial encrypted audio chunks are padded with silence so chunk math stays fixed.
- Removed the old packet format definitions from `defs.h`.
I did not edit playback or Python scripts for this pass. `lib/WavPlayback/WavPlayback.h` is still dirty from the earlier shared `WAV_RIFF_HEADER_LENGTH` change, not from this task.
- Rebuilt `thefly_sec1` again after the final retry-path tweak; passed.
- Search confirms old packet/decoder identifiers are gone from code.
One note: `git diff --check` only complains about trailing whitespace in the already-updated `developer-docs/recording-file-format.md`; I left that doc untouched.

- Added shared encrypted WAV chunk sizing in [inc/defs.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:12):
`WAV_ENCRYPTED_AUDIO_PLAINTEXT_LENGTH = 1024`, encrypted audio chunk length, and encrypted RIFF header length.

- Reworked [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:90) so security level 0 still writes normal `.wav`, while security level 1+ writes `.rec` as encrypted WAV-shaped chunks.
- Encrypted `.rec` files now write the RIFF header as the first encrypted chunk, then fixed-size encrypted PCM chunks.
- Final partial encrypted audio chunks are padded with silence so chunk math stays fixed.

I did not edit playback or Python scripts for this pass. `lib/WavPlayback/WavPlayback.h` is still dirty from the earlier shared `WAV_RIFF_HEADER_LENGTH` change, not from this task.
- Rebuilt `thefly_sec1` again after the final retry-path tweak; passed.
- Search confirms old packet/decoder identifiers are gone from code.
One note: `git diff --check` only complains about trailing whitespace in the already-updated `developer-docs/recording-file-format.md`; I left that doc untouched.

user_prompt: In the `ScrollView` currently any item representing an encrypted file is not clickable. Change it so that now, it launches the same playback mechanism, but only if `BUILD_WITH_ENCRYPTED_PLAYBACK` is defined

The playback mechanism, with `BUILD_WITH_ENCRYPTED_PLAYBACK` defined, can use `*.rec` files and have it use slightly different code paths that include decryption. The RIFF header is fixed size. The file size estimates can still be done with the available constants.
The seeking function will need to calculate the best actual file pointer for a given specified time. Do not bother going more precise than a chunk boundary.
agent_reply: `ScrollView` now routes `.rec` items through the existing playback callback only when `BUILD_WITH_ENCRYPTED_PLAYBACK` is defined: [ScrollView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ScrollView/ScrollView.cpp:1236).
- Decrypts the fixed-size encrypted RIFF header.
- Seeks to the nearest encrypted audio chunk boundary.
- Decrypts audio chunks on demand before mixing/queueing audio.

I exposed the recorder buffers through small accessors in [AudioFileRecorder.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.h:56), backed by the existing `g_wav_plaintext_audio` and `g_wav_encrypted_audio` buffers in [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:1283), so playback doesnâ€™t allocate another large pair.
Both passed; the second sec1 build was after a final state-reset fix in the decrypt setup path.

user_prompt: during recording, track the number of overflow events in either FIFO, an event is overflow flag going up
when the recording is finished, if this number is more than 0, after the text "has been recorded", add a message saying how many

I just did a bluetooth recording, which should be the highest load the system faces, and it recorded... ok... I think? there were some very short events that I am not sure if it is a encoder problem or if my voicemail just sucks
agent_reply: `AudioFileRecorder` now tracks FIFO overflow rising edges on the two recording FIFOs and resets the count at `startRecording()`. The finished-recording modal now keeps the normal message when clean, and adds a line when needed:
It counts per FIFO flag rising edge, so if both recording FIFOs overflow in the same interval, that is 2 events.

user_prompt: work on `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp` only, make sure it follows the guideline at `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md`
agent_reply: I reorganized it around `developer-docs/code-layout-policy.md`: section headers, private prototype block near the top, public/main recorder flow first, then diagnostics/state accessors, then private supporting functions. I kept the function bodies intact and did not intentionally change behavior.
Note: `git status` also shows existing/generated dirt in `lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.h` and `src/version.c`; I did not edit the header, and the build can regenerate `version.c`.

user_prompt: Create a file `lib\FlyGuiViews\ScrollView\FileScrollItem.cpp` and its associated header file.
It is configured with the file name

The file name (including extension) is hashed very quickly into a uint32_t number, this number should be deterministic based on the file name, and the purpose is so that the items in the `ScrollView` donâ€™t all look identical.
For every file, based on the hash number, pick between `sprite_fileback_blue` or `sprite_fileback_yellow` as the backdrop of the icon. Both of these are 100x100 in size.

Now look at the file name, the starting letter should be an indicator of the recording context, such as a call/meeting, or memo, or some subtype of memo. These will pick a 50x50 icon to be overlayed on top of the background icon
For calls, meetings, plain memos, and unknowns, chose from:
Enforce a rule that is `sprite_fileback_yellow` can never be used together with `sprite_filetop_record`
There is no graphic indicator as to whether or not the file is encrypted or not, the file extension doesnâ€™t matter in terms of visuals.
The drawing should also behave like before, where inactive items are drawn as faded. So simply draw both the background icon and the overlay icon in the same faded way.

Wire this into the `bool ScrollView::populateFiles()` and I am hoping this is easy. Firmware update and unknown file logic stay the same. (if I understand correctly, they wonâ€™t even show up now that we are filtering them out from the file list provider)
Please follow `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md` when writing the code
agent_reply: Implemented `FileScrollItem` and wired it into the file scroll list.

Updated [ScrollView.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ScrollView/ScrollView.cpp:250) so playable file entries use the new class, while firmware and unknown fallback sprites stay on the old path. The new item hashes the full filename, chooses blue/yellow backdrops, maps filename prefixes to the requested 50x50 overlays, and avoids the yellow + record combination. Existing faded drawing behavior is handled by the base sprite+overlay path.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec1` timed out after 5 minutes, but `FileScrollItem.cpp` did compile into that env before timeout.
- `git diff --check` passed for the tracked ScrollView edits.
